name: Pernambuco (Brazil)

colors.symbolism: the blood shed in the Pernambucan revolts

description: A blue upper field over a white lower field; the upper bears a single small yellow star above a tricoloured rainbow (red, yellow and green) and a yellow sun, while the white lower field is charged with a red Latin cross.
history: Originally raised by the rebels of the 1817 Pernambucan Revolt against Portuguese rule; reused by the short-lived Confederation of the Equator in 1824, and adopted as the official flag of the State of Pernambuco by State Law No. 1.272 of 25 March 1917.

features.symbolism: Blue upper field for the heavens charged with a star, sun and rainbow; white lower field for peace bearing a red cross
the rainbow as a sign of peace and reconciliation
the Catholic faith and Christian heritage of the captaincy
